# DeepCoin.Net Full LLM Context

> Detailed AI/LLM reference for generating correct C#/.NET code with DeepCoin.Net. Use this file when `llms.txt` is too brief and the task needs concrete examples, endpoint routing guidance, or anti-hallucination rules.

DeepCoin.Net is a strongly typed .NET client library for the DeepCoin REST and WebSocket APIs. It is part of the CryptoExchange.Net ecosystem and uses the same result, shared API, logging, rate limiting, dependency injection, order book, tracker, and socket patterns as the other JKorf exchange libraries.

Current package version in this repository: 4.2.0.
CryptoExchange.Net dependency: 12.2.0.
Target frameworks: netstandard2.0, netstandard2.1, net8.0, net9.0, net10.0.
Native AOT: supported on compatible .NET targets.

Primary documentation:
- README.md: overview, installation, feature table, release notes, basic examples
- docs site: https://cryptoexchange.jkorf.dev/DeepCoin.Net/
- API quick map: `docs/ai-api-map.md`
- source interfaces: `DeepCoin.Net/Interfaces/Clients/**`
- AI examples: `Examples/ai-friendly/`
- assistant skill: `CLAUDE.md`

## Golden Rules For AI Code Generation

- Use `DeepCoinRestClient` for REST and `DeepCoinSocketClient` for WebSocket. Do not generate raw `HttpClient` calls to DeepCoin endpoints.
- Use `DeepCoinCredentials`, not generic `ApiCredentials`, for API key setup. DeepCoin credentials need key, secret, and passphrase.
- Include `using DeepCoin.Net;` when using `DeepCoinCredentials` or `DeepCoinEnvironment`.
- Always check `.Success` before reading `.Data`.
- REST methods return `HttpResult<T>` or `HttpResult`.
- WebSocket subscriptions return `WebSocketResult<UpdateSubscription>`.
- Shared non-I/O symbol/cache helper methods can return `ExchangeCallResult<T>`.
- Reuse clients. Do not instantiate a REST or socket client per request in production code.
- Prefer dependency injection for long-running services.
- Store WebSocket subscriptions and unsubscribe during shutdown.
- Do not call `.Result` or `.Wait()` on async methods.
- Do not invent endpoint names. If unsure, inspect `DeepCoin.Net/Interfaces/Clients/**`.
- Do not invent Binance-style branches. DeepCoin has one `ExchangeApi` root.
- Native DeepCoin methods use hyphenated symbols such as `ETH-USDT` and `ETH-USDT-SWAP`.
- For multi-exchange code, use `CryptoExchange.Net.SharedApis` interfaces from `.ExchangeApi.SharedClient` and call `.Discover()` to inspect supported features.

## Installation

```bash
dotnet add package DeepCoin.Net
```

## Required Usings

Typical REST/trading code:

```csharp
using DeepCoin.Net;
using DeepCoin.Net.Clients;
using DeepCoin.Net.Enums;
```

Shared API code:

```csharp
using DeepCoin.Net.Clients;
using CryptoExchange.Net.SharedApis;
```

Retry/error helper code may also need:

```csharp
using CryptoExchange.Net.Objects;
```

Dependency injection setup:

```csharp
using DeepCoin.Net;
using Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection;
```

## Client Setup

Public market data does not require credentials:

```csharp
var client = new DeepCoinRestClient();

var tickers = await client.ExchangeApi.ExchangeData.GetTickersAsync(SymbolType.Spot);
if (!tickers.Success)
{
    Console.WriteLine(tickers.Error);
    return;
}

var eth = tickers.Data.FirstOrDefault(x => x.Symbol == "ETH-USDT");
Console.WriteLine(eth?.LastPrice);
```

Private account and trading endpoints require credentials:

```csharp
var client = new DeepCoinRestClient(options =>
{
    options.ApiCredentials = new DeepCoinCredentials("API_KEY", "API_SECRET", "API_PASS");
});
```

Socket client with credentials:

```csharp
var socketClient = new DeepCoinSocketClient(options =>
{
    options.ApiCredentials = new DeepCoinCredentials("API_KEY", "API_SECRET", "API_PASS");
});
```

Environments:

```csharp
var live = new DeepCoinRestClient(options => options.Environment = DeepCoinEnvironment.Live);
var customEnvironment = DeepCoinEnvironment.CreateCustom("custom", "https://rest.example", "wss://socket.example");
```

The source exposes `DeepCoinEnvironment.Live` and `DeepCoinEnvironment.CreateCustom(...)`. Do not invent a built-in testnet environment.

## Dependency Injection

Current DI shape:

```csharp
services.AddDeepCoin(options =>
{
    options.ApiCredentials = new DeepCoinCredentials("API_KEY", "API_SECRET", "API_PASS");
});
```

Inject:

```csharp
using DeepCoin.Net.Interfaces.Clients;

public class MyService
{
    private readonly IDeepCoinRestClient _restClient;
    private readonly IDeepCoinSocketClient _socketClient;

    public MyService(IDeepCoinRestClient restClient, IDeepCoinSocketClient socketClient)
    {
        _restClient = restClient;
        _socketClient = socketClient;
    }
}
```

DI also registers `IDeepCoinOrderBookFactory`, `IDeepCoinTrackerFactory`, `ITrackerFactory`, `IDeepCoinUserClientProvider`, and the shared REST/socket interfaces.

## Result Handling Pattern

Always branch on `.Success`.

```csharp
var result = await client.ExchangeApi.ExchangeData.GetTickersAsync(SymbolType.Spot);
if (!result.Success)
{
    Console.WriteLine($"Code: {result.Error?.Code}");
    Console.WriteLine($"Message: {result.Error?.Message}");
    return;
}

var ticker = result.Data.FirstOrDefault(x => x.Symbol == "ETH-USDT");
Console.WriteLine(ticker?.LastPrice);
```

For retry logic, only retry transient errors:

```csharp
async Task<HttpResult<T>> WithRetry<T>(Func<Task<HttpResult<T>>> call, int maxAttempts = 3)
{
    HttpResult<T> last = default!;

    for (var attempt = 1; attempt <= maxAttempts; attempt++)
    {
        last = await call();
        if (last.Success)
            return last;

        if (last.Error?.IsTransient != true)
            return last;

        await Task.Delay(TimeSpan.FromMilliseconds(250 * Math.Pow(2, attempt)));
    }

    return last;
}
```

## Main API Surface

REST:

```text
DeepCoinRestClient
  .ExchangeApi
    .ExchangeData
    .Account
    .Trading
    .SharedClient
```

WebSocket:

```text
DeepCoinSocketClient
  .ExchangeApi
    SubscribeToSymbolUpdatesAsync
    SubscribeToTradeUpdatesAsync
    SubscribeToKlineUpdatesAsync
    SubscribeToOrderBookUpdatesAsync
    SubscribeToUserDataUpdatesAsync
    .SharedClient
```

Important: spot and swap/futures are selected by symbol and `SymbolType`/`TradeMode` parameters, not by separate top-level client branches.

## Intent To Method Map

For a broader quick reference, see `docs/ai-api-map.md`.

Common REST tasks:

```text
Get spot tickers -> client.ExchangeApi.ExchangeData.GetTickersAsync(SymbolType.Spot)
Get swap tickers -> client.ExchangeApi.ExchangeData.GetTickersAsync(SymbolType.Swap)
Get symbols -> client.ExchangeApi.ExchangeData.GetSymbolsAsync(SymbolType.Spot)
Get klines -> client.ExchangeApi.ExchangeData.GetKlinesAsync("ETH-USDT", KlineInterval.OneMinute)
Get order book -> client.ExchangeApi.ExchangeData.GetOrderBookAsync("ETH-USDT", depth: 25)
Get funding rates -> client.ExchangeApi.ExchangeData.GetFundingRateAsync(ProductGroup.USDTMargined, "ETH-USDT-SWAP")
Get balances -> client.ExchangeApi.Account.GetBalancesAsync(SymbolType.Spot)
Set leverage -> client.ExchangeApi.Account.SetLeverageAsync("ETH-USDT-SWAP", 5m, TradeMode.Cross, PositionType.Split)
Get deposit history -> client.ExchangeApi.Account.GetDepositHistoryAsync("USDT")
Get withdraw history -> client.ExchangeApi.Account.GetWithdrawHistoryAsync("USDT")
Start user stream -> client.ExchangeApi.Account.StartUserStreamAsync()
Keep alive user stream -> client.ExchangeApi.Account.KeepAliveUserStreamAsync(listenKey)
Get positions -> client.ExchangeApi.Trading.GetPositionsAsync(SymbolType.Swap, "ETH-USDT-SWAP")
Place order -> client.ExchangeApi.Trading.PlaceOrderAsync(...)
Edit order -> client.ExchangeApi.Trading.EditOrderAsync(...)
Cancel order -> client.ExchangeApi.Trading.CancelOrderAsync(symbol, orderId)
Cancel multiple orders -> client.ExchangeApi.Trading.CancelOrdersAsync(orderIds)
Cancel all orders -> client.ExchangeApi.Trading.CancelAllOrdersAsync(...)
Get open order -> client.ExchangeApi.Trading.GetOpenOrderAsync(symbol, orderId)
Get closed order -> client.ExchangeApi.Trading.GetClosedOrderAsync(symbol, orderId)
Get open orders -> client.ExchangeApi.Trading.GetOpenOrdersAsync(symbol)
Get closed orders -> client.ExchangeApi.Trading.GetClosedOrdersAsync(SymbolType.Swap, symbol)
Get user trades -> client.ExchangeApi.Trading.GetUserTradesAsync(SymbolType.Spot, "ETH-USDT")
Set TP/SL -> client.ExchangeApi.Trading.SetTpSlAsync(orderId, takeProfitTriggerPrice, stopLossTriggerPrice)
```

Common WebSocket tasks:

```text
Subscribe ticker -> socketClient.ExchangeApi.SubscribeToSymbolUpdatesAsync(symbol, handler)
Subscribe trades -> socketClient.ExchangeApi.SubscribeToTradeUpdatesAsync(symbol, handler)
Subscribe one-minute klines -> socketClient.ExchangeApi.SubscribeToKlineUpdatesAsync(symbol, handler)
Subscribe order book -> socketClient.ExchangeApi.SubscribeToOrderBookUpdatesAsync(symbol, handler)
Subscribe user data -> socketClient.ExchangeApi.SubscribeToUserDataUpdatesAsync(listenKey, ...)
```

## Spot Order Example

```csharp
using DeepCoin.Net;
using DeepCoin.Net.Clients;
using DeepCoin.Net.Enums;

var client = new DeepCoinRestClient(options =>
{
    options.ApiCredentials = new DeepCoinCredentials("API_KEY", "API_SECRET", "API_PASS");
});

var order = await client.ExchangeApi.Trading.PlaceOrderAsync(
    symbol: "ETH-USDT",
    side: OrderSide.Buy,
    orderType: OrderType.Limit,
    quantity: 0.1m,
    price: 2000m,
    tradeMode: TradeMode.Spot);

if (!order.Success)
{
    Console.WriteLine(order.Error);
    return;
}

Console.WriteLine(order.Data.OrderId);
```

For market buy orders that specify quote currency amount, use `quantityType: QuantityType.QuoteAsset`.

## Swap/Futures Order Example

```csharp
using DeepCoin.Net;
using DeepCoin.Net.Clients;
using DeepCoin.Net.Enums;

var client = new DeepCoinRestClient(options =>
{
    options.ApiCredentials = new DeepCoinCredentials("API_KEY", "API_SECRET", "API_PASS");
});

var leverage = await client.ExchangeApi.Account.SetLeverageAsync(
    "ETH-USDT-SWAP",
    5m,
    TradeMode.Cross,
    PositionType.Split);
if (!leverage.Success)
{
    Console.WriteLine(leverage.Error);
    return;
}

var order = await client.ExchangeApi.Trading.PlaceOrderAsync(
    symbol: "ETH-USDT-SWAP",
    side: OrderSide.Buy,
    orderType: OrderType.Market,
    quantity: 0.1m,
    tradeMode: TradeMode.Cross,
    positionSide: PositionSide.Long);
```

Use `reduceOnly: true` when closing a swap/futures position if you want to avoid flipping the position.

## WebSocket Subscription Example

```csharp
using DeepCoin.Net.Clients;

var socketClient = new DeepCoinSocketClient();

var tickerSub = await socketClient.ExchangeApi.SubscribeToSymbolUpdatesAsync(
    "ETH-USDT",
    update => Console.WriteLine(update.Data.LastPrice));

if (!tickerSub.Success)
{
    Console.WriteLine(tickerSub.Error);
    return;
}

Console.ReadLine();
await socketClient.UnsubscribeAsync(tickerSub.Data);
```

Authenticated stream pattern:

```csharp
using DeepCoin.Net;
using DeepCoin.Net.Clients;

var restClient = new DeepCoinRestClient(options =>
{
    options.ApiCredentials = new DeepCoinCredentials("API_KEY", "API_SECRET", "API_PASS");
});

var listenKey = await restClient.ExchangeApi.Account.StartUserStreamAsync();
if (!listenKey.Success)
{
    Console.WriteLine(listenKey.Error);
    return;
}

var userSub = await socketClient.ExchangeApi.SubscribeToUserDataUpdatesAsync(
    listenKey.Data.ListenKey,
    onOrderMessage: update => Console.WriteLine($"Orders: {update.Data.Length}"));
```

## SharedApis Multi-Exchange Pattern

```csharp
using DeepCoin.Net.Clients;
using CryptoExchange.Net.SharedApis;

var restClient = new DeepCoinRestClient();
ISpotTickerRestClient tickerClient = restClient.ExchangeApi.SharedClient;
var symbol = new SharedSymbol(TradingMode.Spot, "ETH", "USDT");
var info = restClient.ExchangeApi.SharedClient.Discover();
var supportedFeatures = info.Features.Where(x => x.Supported).Select(x => x.EndpointName);

var ticker = await tickerClient.GetSpotTickerAsync(new GetTickerRequest(symbol));
if (!ticker.Success)
{
    Console.WriteLine(ticker.Error);
    return;
}

Console.WriteLine(ticker.Data.LastPrice);
```

`ISpotSymbolRestClient.SpotSymbolCatalog` and `IFuturesSymbolRestClient.FuturesSymbolCatalog` expose the shared symbol catalogs. `GetSpotSymbolsAsync` and `GetFuturesSymbolsAsync` return symbols with `DisplayName`, `BaseAssetType`, `BaseAssetSubType`, `QuoteAssetType`, and `QuoteAssetSubType`; `GetSymbolsRequest` can filter by those asset classifications. DeepCoin marks spot assets as crypto (including recognized stablecoin subtypes), inverse-futures quote assets as fiat, linear-futures quote assets as crypto stablecoins, and recognized commodity/equity futures bases as TradFi.

Shared WebSocket subscriptions return an `UpdateSubscription` in `.Data`. For shared interface variables, unsubscribe through the concrete socket client:

```csharp
var socketClient = new DeepCoinSocketClient();
ITickerSocketClient tickerSocket = socketClient.ExchangeApi.SharedClient;

var sub = await tickerSocket.SubscribeToTickerUpdatesAsync(
    new SubscribeTickerRequest(new SharedSymbol(TradingMode.Spot, "ETH", "USDT")),
    update => Console.WriteLine(update.Data.LastPrice));

if (!sub.Success)
{
    Console.WriteLine(sub.Error);
    return;
}

Console.ReadLine();
await socketClient.UnsubscribeAsync(sub.Data);
```

Do not call `tickerSocket.UnsubscribeAsync(...)` on `ITickerSocketClient`; that shared interface does not expose the method.

## Symbol And Precision Handling

Before placing user-driven orders, query symbol metadata with `GetSymbolsAsync`.

```csharp
var symbols = await client.ExchangeApi.ExchangeData.GetSymbolsAsync(SymbolType.Spot, symbol: "ETH-USDT");
if (!symbols.Success || symbols.Data.Length == 0)
{
    Console.WriteLine(symbols.Error);
    return;
}

var symbolInfo = symbols.Data.First();
```

Use the fields exposed by `DeepCoinSymbol` and the exchange documentation to validate order quantities/prices. Do not use ad hoc string manipulation or assume Binance-style filters exist.

## Error Handling Notes

DeepCoin.Net returns API, network, and rate-limit errors through `.Error` on result objects. It generally does not throw for normal API failures. Exceptions are for misconfiguration, disposal, cancellation, or programming errors.

Common DeepCoin error categories:

```text
Authentication/signature: API key, secret, passphrase, permissions, environment, or signing issue.
Rate limit/service unavailable: usually transient; back off and retry if result.Error.IsTransient is true.
Invalid symbol/account type: use DeepCoin native symbol format and the correct SymbolType.
Invalid order price/quantity: query symbols and validate request values before placing orders.
Insufficient balance: permanent for the current account state; surface to the caller.
Order not found: may be expected for already-filled, canceled, or account/symbol mismatched orders.
```

## AI-Friendly Examples

Use these examples when generating code:

```text
Examples/ai-friendly/01-spot-quickstart.cs
  Client setup, public ticker, authenticated balance, limit spot order, order status, cancellation.

Examples/ai-friendly/02-futures.cs
  Swap/futures leverage, market order, position lookup, reduce-only close.

Examples/ai-friendly/03-websocket.cs
  Public ticker, trade, kline, order book, authenticated user data stream, success checks, teardown.

Examples/ai-friendly/04-multi-exchange.cs
  CryptoExchange.Net SharedApis REST/socket patterns and capability discovery.

Examples/ai-friendly/05-error-handling.cs
  HttpResult, WebSocketResult, ExchangeCallResult patterns; transient retry; DeepCoin error categories; symbol validation.
```

These files are intended to be copied into a console `Program.cs` after `dotnet add package DeepCoin.Net`.

## Anti-Hallucination Checklist

Before finalizing generated code:

- Does it compile with the current package?
- Does it include `using DeepCoin.Net;` when using credentials or environments?
- Does every REST/socket result check `.Success` before `.Data`?
- Does it use the correct API branch: `ExchangeApi.ExchangeData`, `ExchangeApi.Account`, or `ExchangeApi.Trading`?
- Does it avoid raw DeepCoin URLs and manual signing?
- Does it avoid invented `DeepCoinClient`, `SpotApi`, `UsdFuturesApi`, and `CoinFuturesApi` APIs?
- Does it use `DeepCoinCredentials("key", "secret", "pass")`?
- Does native DeepCoin code use symbols such as `ETH-USDT` or `ETH-USDT-SWAP`?
- Does it avoid `.Result` and `.Wait()`?
- Are WebSocket subscriptions unsubscribed on shutdown?
- For private WebSocket code, does it request and check a listen key first?
- For shared socket interfaces, does it keep the concrete socket client for `UnsubscribeAsync(sub.Data)`?
- For swap/futures directional orders, does it include the intended `positionSide` and `tradeMode`?

## Source Of Truth For Method Names

When unsure, inspect these files instead of guessing:

```text
DeepCoin.Net/Interfaces/Clients/IDeepCoinRestClient.cs
DeepCoin.Net/Interfaces/Clients/IDeepCoinSocketClient.cs
DeepCoin.Net/Interfaces/Clients/ExchangeApi/IDeepCoinRestClientExchangeApi.cs
DeepCoin.Net/Interfaces/Clients/ExchangeApi/IDeepCoinRestClientExchangeApiExchangeData.cs
DeepCoin.Net/Interfaces/Clients/ExchangeApi/IDeepCoinRestClientExchangeApiAccount.cs
DeepCoin.Net/Interfaces/Clients/ExchangeApi/IDeepCoinRestClientExchangeApiTrading.cs
DeepCoin.Net/Interfaces/Clients/ExchangeApi/IDeepCoinSocketClientExchangeApi.cs
DeepCoin.Net/Interfaces/Clients/ExchangeApi/IDeepCoinRestClientExchangeApiShared.cs
DeepCoin.Net/Interfaces/Clients/ExchangeApi/IDeepCoinSocketClientExchangeApiShared.cs
```

Prefer the interface files over memory when generating endpoint names, parameters, callback names, and return types.

## Common Wrong Patterns To Avoid

Wrong:

```csharp
var client = new DeepCoinClient();
```

Use:

```csharp
var client = new DeepCoinRestClient();
```

Wrong:

```csharp
options.ApiCredentials = new ApiCredentials("key", "secret");
```

Use:

```csharp
options.ApiCredentials = new DeepCoinCredentials("key", "secret", "pass");
```

Wrong:

```csharp
client.SpotApi.ExchangeData.GetTickerAsync("ETHUSDT");
```

Use:

```csharp
client.ExchangeApi.ExchangeData.GetTickersAsync(SymbolType.Spot);
```

Wrong:

```csharp
var result = await client.ExchangeApi.ExchangeData.GetTickersAsync(SymbolType.Spot);
Console.WriteLine(result.Data.First().LastPrice);
```

Use:

```csharp
var result = await client.ExchangeApi.ExchangeData.GetTickersAsync(SymbolType.Spot);
if (!result.Success)
{
    Console.WriteLine(result.Error);
    return;
}
Console.WriteLine(result.Data.First().LastPrice);
```

Wrong:

```csharp
await sharedTickerSocket.UnsubscribeAsync(sub.Data);
```

Use:

```csharp
await socketClient.UnsubscribeAsync(sub.Data);
```
